meta.title: Arctic under-ice bloom
meta.lesson: Each spring, as Arctic sea ice thins and melt ponds form, sunlight finally reaches the water below and triggers a massive bloom of ice-associated diatoms in the thin sunlit layer just under the ice. Below that photic zone the water is cold, dark, and nutrient-rich. Your lineage lives in this stratified column: stay near the bright surface to feed while the bloom lasts, because the deep is a cold, lightless refuge with little to eat.
